title: VoIP debug endpoint exposes API keys in production
description: The /api/voip/debug route exposes partial Telnyx API key and connection ID. Unlike community/debug which has a NODE_ENV guard, this route has NO production check — it's accessible to anyone in production.
fix: Add `if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'development') return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 });` at the top of the handler, or delete the route entirely as the comment says 'DELETE THIS after debugging'.

title: All 10 v1 API routes lack tenant isolation
description: All v1 API routes (customers, products, invoices, orders, inventory, webhooks) use withApiAuth but have NO tenantId filtering. In a multi-tenant system, API key holders from tenant A can access data from tenant B. Routes: customers, customers/[id], products, products/[id], invoices, invoices/[id], orders, orders/[id], inventory, webhooks.
fix: Add tenantId filtering to all v1 API route queries. The apiKey object should include tenantId, and all Prisma where clauses should include `tenantId: apiKey.tenantId`.

title: Platform health endpoint exposes tenant data without auth
description: GET /api/platform/health?tenant=<slug> is fully public (no auth check). It exposes: tenant name, status, plan, creation date, Stripe subscription status, user count, product count, order count. Anyone can enumerate all tenants and their business metrics.
fix: Add super-admin auth check for tenant-specific health queries. The global health endpoint can remain public, but tenant-specific data must require authentication.

title: LMS routes lack CSRF protection (3 mutation endpoints)
description: POST /api/lms/enroll, POST /api/lms/progress, POST /api/lms/quiz — all accept mutations from authenticated users but have no CSRF token validation. A malicious site could enroll users in courses, manipulate progress, or submit quiz answers on their behalf.
fix: Add CSRF validation using validateCsrf() or wrap with withUserGuard which includes CSRF. Apply to all 3 LMS POST routes.

title: LMS routes expose raw error.message to clients
description: All 3 LMS mutation routes (enroll, progress, quiz) catch errors and return `error.message` directly to the client: `return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 400 })`. This can leak internal implementation details, database schema info, or Prisma error messages.
fix: Return a generic error message to the client and log the detailed error server-side: `logger.error('LMS error', { error: error.message }); return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Operation failed' }, { status: 400 });`

title: LMS courses catalog has weak tenant isolation
description: GET /api/lms/courses uses getCurrentTenantIdFromContext() but falls back to `{}` (no filter) if tenantId is missing: `...(tenantId ? { tenantId } : {})`. This means if tenant context is lost, ALL courses from ALL tenants are returned.
fix: Require tenantId or return empty results: `if (!tenantId) return NextResponse.json({ courses: [], total: 0 });`

title: XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without DOMPurify in tutoriels page
description: The admin tutoriels page renders markdown content via a custom markdownToHtml() function and outputs it with dangerouslySetInnerHTML WITHOUT DOMPurify sanitization. While the markdownToHtml function does HTML-entity escaping first, the custom regex-based parser could have edge cases that allow XSS if markdown content is user-controllable or injected.
fix: Add DOMPurify.sanitize() around the renderedHtml output: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: DOMPurify.sanitize(renderedHtml) }}`

title: PII logged: user email in forgot-password info log
description: Line 62: `logger.info('Password reset requested for non-existent email: ${email}')` logs the actual email address. Line 113 also logs email in password_reset_requested event. This creates a PII trail in production logs that may violate Loi 25/GDPR.
fix: Hash or mask the email in logs: `logger.info('Password reset requested for non-existent email', { emailHash: createHash('sha256').update(email).digest('hex').slice(0,12) })`

title: PII logged: user email in marketing email send log
description: Line 235: `logger.info('Marketing email sent: ${emailType} to ${user.email}')` logs the recipient email. Line 196 in send-order-email also logs user.email. These create unnecessary PII trails.
fix: Log user ID instead of email: `logger.info('Marketing email sent', { type: emailType, userId: user.id })`

title: PII logged: user email in admin password reset log
description: Line 76: `logger.info('Admin-initiated password reset', { userId: id, email: user.email })` logs the user email alongside userId. The userId alone is sufficient for audit purposes.
fix: Remove email from log: `logger.info('Admin-initiated password reset', { userId: id, initiatedBy: session.user.id })`

title: PII logged: user email in replenishment reminder cron
description: Line 348: logs `user.email` and product name in cron job output. Production cron logs should not contain customer email addresses.
fix: Replace email with userId in log output.

title: PII logged: session user email in Stripe sync log
description: Line 109: `logger.info('Stripe sync started:', { syncId, startDate, endDate, initiatedBy: session.user.id || session.user.email })` falls back to logging email if user ID is missing.
fix: Remove email fallback: `initiatedBy: session.user.id || 'unknown'`

title: SQL injection risk: $queryRawUnsafe with string interpolation in tenantId injection
description: The tenantQueryRaw and tenantExecuteRaw functions use $queryRawUnsafe with string concatenation in injectTenantFilter(). While tenantId is parameterized ($N), the SQL string manipulation via regex could be exploited if the input SQL contains specific patterns that confuse the regex (e.g., SQL with string literals containing 'WHERE' or 'GROUP BY').
fix: Use Prisma.sql tagged templates with Prisma.$queryRaw instead of $queryRawUnsafe. If raw queries are necessary, use a proper SQL parser instead of regex for clause injection.

title: Platform provision route lacks CSRF and rate limiting
description: POST /api/platform/provision creates new tenants and user accounts. It relies only on Stripe session verification but has no CSRF token, no rate limiting. A replay attack with a valid session ID could cause issues.
fix: Add rate limiting by IP. Consider adding idempotency key based on sessionId to prevent double-provisioning.

title: Platform onboarding route lacks rate limiting and has 6 writes without transaction
description: POST /api/platform/onboarding has 6 Prisma writes with no $transaction wrapper and no rate limiting. The comment says 'no login required yet — owner just created account'. Race conditions could leave onboarding in an inconsistent state.
fix: Wrap all writes in prisma.$transaction() and add rate limiting.

title: 16 VoIP routes use auth() but lack CSRF protection
description: VoIP routes (wrap-up, dncl, park, park/retrieve, crm, flip, pickup, dialer, calendar-sync, agent-assist, presence, scripts, scripts/[id], ivr, ivr/[id], coaching/[id]) use auth() for authentication but have no CSRF token validation on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE operations.
fix: Wrap VoIP mutation handlers with withAdminGuard or add explicit validateCsrf() calls.

title: Demo request endpoint lacks rate limiting
description: POST /api/demo-request accepts form submissions without rate limiting. An attacker could spam thousands of demo requests, polluting the CRM database and potentially triggering email floods.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware(ip, '/api/demo-request') at the top of the handler.

title: Cart share endpoint lacks CSRF and rate limiting
description: POST /api/cart/share allows sharing carts without CSRF protection or rate limiting. Could be abused for spam or to create excessive shared cart entries.
fix: Add CSRF validation and rate limiting.

title: Data deletion endpoint lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/data-deletion handles GDPR/Loi 25 data deletion requests without CSRF validation. A CSRF attack could trigger unwanted data deletion for an authenticated user.
fix: Add validateCsrf() call or wrap with withUserGuard.

title: Email preferences endpoint lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/email-preferences allows changing email preferences without CSRF validation. An attacker could silently unsubscribe users from important notifications.
fix: Add CSRF validation.

title: Accept-terms endpoint lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/auth/accept-terms records legal consent without CSRF validation. A CSRF attack could forge consent acceptance on behalf of users.
fix: Add CSRF validation — this is especially important for legal consent recording.

title: MD5 used for cache keys (weak hash)
description: Four locations use createHash('md5') for cache keys: products/search, products, auto-translate, api-response. While MD5 for cache keys isn't a direct security vulnerability, it's a weak algorithm and using it normalizes its use in the codebase.
fix: Replace with createHash('sha256') for all cache key generation. Minimal performance impact with much better collision resistance.

title: CORS missing Access-Control-Allow-Credentials header
description: The middleware sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin, Allow-Methods, and Allow-Headers but never sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials. This may cause issues with authenticated cross-origin requests and cookie-based sessions in multi-tenant subdomain setup.
fix: Add `response.headers.set('Access-Control-Allow-Credentials', 'true')` when origin matches a tenant domain.

title: Auth mutation routes missing Content-Type validation
description: 6 auth routes (signup, register, login, forgot-password, reset-password, demo-request) accept POST without validating Content-Type. Missing Content-Type check allows potential type confusion attacks.
fix: Add `validateContentType(request)` check at the beginning of each POST handler.

title: Path traversal risk in tutorial slug route despite sanitization
description: The route allows '/' in slugPath (regex: /^[\w\-/]+$/) and uses path.join(process.cwd(), 'docs', 'user-guide', slugPath). While path.join normalizes '..' on most platforms, allowing '/' enables accessing any file within the user-guide directory tree. No auth check means anyone can read these files.
fix: Add auth check (withAdminGuard or session check). Additional defense: resolve the path and verify it starts with the expected base directory.

title: Certificate verification endpoint lacks rate limiting
description: GET /api/lms/certificates/verify is public and has no rate limiting. An attacker could brute-force certificate codes to enumerate valid certificates and student information.
fix: Add rate limiting to prevent enumeration attacks.

title: LMS enrollment has no rate limiting
description: POST /api/lms/enroll has no rate limiting. A user could rapidly enroll in hundreds of courses, potentially causing database bloat or abuse of free course access.
fix: Add rate limiting: `await rateLimitMiddleware(ip, '/api/lms/enroll', session.user.id)`

title: Password comparison via === in Zod refine (not timing-safe)
description: Line 27: `.refine((data) => data.password === data.confirmPassword)` uses === for password comparison. While this is client-submitted data comparison (not a secret comparison), it creates a pattern that developers might copy for actual secret comparisons.
fix: Low priority since this compares two user-submitted fields, not server secrets. Consider using timingSafeEqual for consistency.

title: 182 API routes lack rate limiting
description: Out of 936 API routes, 182 have no rate limiting mechanism (neither via withAdminGuard, withUserGuard, nor explicit rateLimit calls). While some are GET-only or webhook endpoints, many accept mutations that could be abused.
fix: Audit all 182 routes and add appropriate rate limiting. Priority: public-facing mutation endpoints first, then admin endpoints.

title: 69 mutation routes lack CSRF protection
description: 69 routes export POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE handlers but have no CSRF validation via withAdminGuard, withUserGuard, validateCsrf, withMobileGuard, withApiAuth, or webhook secret verification. This includes VoIP, LMS, platform, cart, and consent routes.
fix: Categorize routes: (1) webhooks from external services don't need CSRF, (2) auth endpoints use different protection, (3) all other mutation endpoints need CSRF. Add appropriate protection to each category.

title: 30+ routes with multiple writes lack database transactions
description: Over 30 route handlers perform 3+ Prisma write operations (create/update/delete) without wrapping them in $transaction. This creates race conditions where partial writes can leave data in an inconsistent state. Worst offenders: admin/emails/campaigns/[id]/send (11 writes), admin/scraper/jobs (9 writes), cron/retry-webhooks (9 writes), platform/onboarding (6 writes).
fix: Wrap multi-write operations in prisma.$transaction([...]). Priority: payment-related, onboarding, and email campaign routes.

title: V1 API has no versioning strategy — no v2 exists
description: The v1 API exists with 6 resources (customers, products, invoices, orders, inventory, webhooks) but there is no v2 API and no API versioning strategy. Breaking changes to v1 would affect all API consumers with no migration path.
fix: Document an API versioning strategy. Consider header-based versioning (Accept: application/vnd.koraline.v1+json) as an alternative to path-based.

title: 55 of 936 API routes set Cache-Control headers (94% uncached)
description: Only 55 out of 936 API routes explicitly set Cache-Control headers. The remaining 881 routes rely on browser/CDN defaults, which may cache sensitive authenticated responses. API responses containing user data should explicitly set 'Cache-Control: no-store, private'.
fix: Add default Cache-Control headers in middleware for all /api/ routes: `no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, private`. Allow individual routes to override with explicit caching.

title: 60+ POST/PUT/PATCH routes lack Zod input validation
description: At least 60 routes that accept POST, PUT, or PATCH requests don't use Zod (or any schema validation). This includes cron routes, webhook handlers, platform routes, admin routes, and email routes. Without validation, malformed input can cause unexpected behavior or errors.
fix: Add Zod schemas to all mutation routes. Priority: public-facing and platform routes first.

title: Multiple accounting findMany queries without pagination
description: Several accounting routes use findMany without take/limit parameters: trial-balance, income-statement, balance-sheet, chart-of-accounts, fiscal-calendar, customer-statements. On tenants with large datasets, these could cause OOM or timeout errors.
fix: Add default pagination (take: 1000) or use streaming/cursor-based pagination for large result sets.

title: GST/QST declaration uses findMany+take:50000 (acknowledged OOM risk)
description: Line 137 comment: 'TODO: Replace findMany+take:50000 with aggregate SQL to avoid OOM on large datasets'. This existing TODO acknowledges the risk but hasn't been fixed.
fix: Replace with aggregate SQL query as the TODO suggests. Use Prisma groupBy or raw SQL aggregation instead of loading 50K records into memory.

title: Export route has acknowledged scalability issue (TODO for streaming)
description: Line 151 comment: 'TODO: Replace with ReadableStream + cursor pagination for truly large exports'. Currently loads all data into memory before sending response.
fix: Implement streaming response with cursor-based pagination for large data exports.

title: Multiple JSON.parse calls without try-catch in accounting routes
description: At least 15 locations in accounting routes call JSON.parse on database fields (resultData, errorLog, inputData, notes, config) without try-catch. If DB data is corrupted, these will throw unhandled exceptions and return 500 errors.
fix: Wrap all JSON.parse calls in try-catch with fallback values: `let data = null; try { data = JSON.parse(str); } catch { data = null; }`

title: 47 pages use useSearchParams/useRouter without Suspense boundaries
description: 47 page.tsx files use client-side hooks (useSearchParams, useRouter, usePathname) without wrapping content in <Suspense> boundaries. In Next.js 14+, this causes the entire page to client-side render instead of streaming SSR. Affected areas: admin/customers, admin/clients, admin/crm (10+ pages), admin/emails, admin/navigateur, and more.
fix: Wrap components using these hooks in <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}> or extract the hook-using code into a separate client component.

title: 22 pages without error.tsx boundaries
description: 22 page directories lack error.tsx error boundaries: onboarding, privacy, signup, terms, admin/abonnement (3 sub-pages), admin/formation (7 sub-pages), admin/parametres/product-types, admin/platform (2 sub-pages), platform (3 sub-pages), pricing. Errors in these pages will bubble up to the nearest parent error boundary or crash the app.
fix: Add error.tsx files to these directories with appropriate error UI and recovery actions.

title: 15 admin pages without loading.tsx (no loading states)
description: 15 admin page directories lack loading.tsx, causing no visual feedback during page transitions. Users see a blank screen or stale content while data loads.
fix: Add loading.tsx with skeleton UI to each admin page directory.

title: Only 13 not-found.tsx pages across the app
description: Only 13 not-found.tsx pages exist for 361 pages. Most dynamic routes ([slug], [id]) that could receive invalid parameters will show a generic Next.js 404 instead of a helpful, branded not-found page.
fix: Add not-found.tsx to key dynamic route directories, especially: admin/[id] routes, shop product/category pages, platform pages.

title: Multiple sequential DB queries in webhook handler instead of batched
description: The Stripe webhook handler performs multiple sequential findMany queries for products, product options, and reservations when processing orders. While it uses Set+Map for deduplication, the multiple await calls in sequence add latency to webhook processing.
fix: Use Promise.all() for independent queries and prisma.$transaction for dependent writes.

title: Polling interval without cleanup in chat components
description: ChatDashboard.tsx line 150 sets a 3-second polling interval. While a ref is used, multiple page navigations without proper cleanup could stack intervals. The chat/page.tsx also has a 3-second interval (line 165) and typing timeout (line 311).
fix: Ensure all intervals are cleared in useEffect cleanup. Consider using SWR/React Query with refetchInterval instead of manual polling.

title: Artificial delay with setTimeout in audit page
description: Line 131: `await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000))` introduces an artificial 1-second delay. This may have been added for UI effect but degrades performance.
fix: Remove the artificial delay or replace with a proper loading state transition.

title: Password reset logs violate Loi 25 PII requirements
description: Under Quebec Loi 25, personal information (including email addresses) must be protected and access logged. The forgot-password route logs the actual email address for both existing and non-existing users, creating an unnecessary PII data trail in application logs.
fix: Use hashed email identifiers in logs instead of raw email addresses.

title: Consent token endpoint lacks CSRF protection
description: POST to /api/consent/[token] records user consent decisions without CSRF protection. Under Loi 25/GDPR, consent must be freely given — a CSRF attack could forge consent acceptance.
fix: Add CSRF validation to consent endpoints to ensure consent is genuine.

title: LMS quiz submission has no anti-cheating measures
description: POST /api/lms/quiz accepts quiz answers without any timing validation, attempt limits, or answer-shuffling. Students can submit answers instantly (no minimum time) and there's no check for concurrent sessions taking the same quiz.
fix: Add: (1) max quiz attempts per enrollment, (2) minimum time between quiz start and submission, (3) single active session per user per quiz.

title: Platform checkout accepts unauthenticated requests
description: POST /api/platform/checkout creates Stripe checkout sessions for new tenants without any authentication. While this is expected for new signups, there's no CAPTCHA, honeypot, or rate limiting to prevent automated abuse.
fix: Add rate limiting per IP and consider CAPTCHA or honeypot fields.

title: Hardcoded English strings in admin UI
description: At least 2 admin components use hardcoded English strings instead of i18n: WebhooksClient.tsx (title='Delete') and crm/workflows/page.tsx (title='Delete'). While top-level keys are synchronized between en.json and fr.json, component-level hardcoded strings bypass translation.
fix: Replace hardcoded strings with t() translations.

title: LMS error messages are hardcoded in English
description: All LMS API routes return hardcoded English error messages: 'Authentication required', 'No tenant context', 'courseId is required', 'Validation failed', 'Enrollment failed', 'Quiz submission failed'. These should use i18n or at least error codes.
fix: Use error codes (e.g., 'AUTH_REQUIRED') and let the frontend translate, or use a server-side i18n function.

title: Tutorial page sidebar buttons lack ARIA labels
description: The tutorial page sidebar uses icon-only buttons for section expansion without aria-label attributes. Screen readers cannot distinguish between sections.
fix: Add aria-label to section toggle buttons: `aria-label={`Toggle ${section.title}`}`

title: LMS routes have zero test coverage
description: The 5 LMS API routes (enroll, progress, quiz, courses, certificates/verify) have no automated tests. These routes handle enrollment, progress tracking, quiz grading, and certificate verification — all critical for LMS functionality.
fix: Create integration tests for all LMS routes covering: enrollment flow, progress updates, quiz submission/grading, course catalog, certificate verification.

title: Platform routes (multi-tenant) have zero test coverage
description: The 10 platform API routes (provision, checkout, onboarding, licenses, modules, domain, subscription, billing-portal, health, webhook) have no automated tests. These are critical for the multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure.
fix: Create integration tests covering: tenant provisioning flow, subscription management, module activation, domain verification.

title: V1 public API routes lack integration tests
description: The public v1 API (customers, products, invoices, orders, inventory, webhooks) has no integration tests despite being an external-facing API that third parties depend on.
fix: Create comprehensive API tests for all v1 endpoints including auth, pagination, filtering, CRUD operations, and error cases.

title: No global API response timeout enforcement
description: There is no global middleware or configuration to enforce API response timeouts. Long-running queries (especially in accounting and export routes) could hold connections indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool.
fix: Add a global timeout middleware or configure Next.js serverless function timeout. Add individual timeouts for known long-running operations.

title: Middleware error handler logs to console.error instead of structured logger
description: Line 362: `console.error(JSON.stringify({ event: 'middleware_getToken_error', ... }))` uses console.error instead of the structured logger. Middleware errors bypass the logging pipeline and may not appear in monitoring systems.
fix: Import and use the structured logger in middleware: `logger.error('middleware_getToken_error', { pathname, error: String(err) })`

title: Health endpoint exposes Stripe and Sentry configuration status
description: The /api/health endpoint reveals whether STRIPE_SECRET_KEY and SENTRY_DSN are configured. This information helps attackers understand the application's infrastructure and payment processing setup.
fix: Remove third-party service status from the public health endpoint. Move detailed health checks to a separate authenticated /api/admin/health endpoint.

title: Signup route has 4 writes without transaction (race condition)
description: The signup route performs 4 Prisma write operations (user creation, email verification, audit log, etc.) without a $transaction wrapper. A race condition could create a user without proper initialization data.
fix: Wrap all signup writes in prisma.$transaction().

title: Estimate token route has 3 writes without transaction and no CSRF
description: POST to /api/estimates/[token] allows clients to accept/decline estimates. It performs 3 writes without $transaction and has no CSRF protection. A CSRF attack could silently accept estimates on behalf of a client.
fix: Add CSRF validation and wrap writes in $transaction.

title: VoIP public route exposes internal telephony configuration
description: The /api/voip/public route is accessible without authentication and may expose internal VoIP configuration data.
fix: Review what data is exposed and add rate limiting at minimum. Consider requiring authentication.

title: Unsubscribe route has 4 writes without transaction
description: POST /api/unsubscribe performs 4 Prisma writes without $transaction. If any write fails mid-operation, the user could be partially unsubscribed, leading to continued unwanted emails.
fix: Wrap all writes in prisma.$transaction().

title: Email campaign send has 11 writes without transaction
description: The email campaign send handler performs 11 Prisma write operations without a $transaction. A failure mid-send could leave the campaign in an inconsistent state — partially sent, incorrectly marked, or with wrong statistics.
fix: Wrap critical writes in $transaction. Use saga pattern for the send loop with proper error recovery.

title: Scraper jobs route has 9 writes without transaction
description: The scraper jobs handler performs 9 Prisma write operations without $transaction. A scraper job could be partially created if any write fails.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Webhook retry cron has 9 writes without transaction
description: The webhook retry cron performs 9 writes without $transaction. Failed retries could leave webhook delivery records in inconsistent states.
fix: Wrap each retry attempt in its own $transaction.

title: Employee management route has 7 writes without transaction
description: Employee CRUD operations perform 7 writes without $transaction. Updating employee details, roles, and permissions should be atomic.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Inbound email webhook has 7 writes without transaction
description: Processing inbound emails involves 7 writes (message creation, thread update, conversation update, etc.) without $transaction. A failure could lose or duplicate email messages.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Email reply handler has 7 writes without transaction
description: Replying to an email involves 7 writes (reply creation, thread update, tracking entries, etc.) without $transaction.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Data retention cron has 7 writes without transaction
description: The data retention cron performs 7 delete/update operations. Partial completion could leave some data deleted while related records remain, violating referential integrity.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction() or use cascading deletes.

title: LMS quiz has no time limit or attempt limit enforcement
description: The quiz submission endpoint accepts answers without checking: (1) if the quiz has a time limit that was exceeded, (2) if the student has exceeded max attempts, (3) if the enrollment is still active.
fix: Add validation in submitQuizAttempt: check enrollment status, check attempt count against quiz.maxAttempts, validate quiz timing.

title: LMS progress update has no completion validation
description: The progress update endpoint accepts isCompleted=true without verifying that the student actually consumed the lesson content (e.g., video not fully watched, minimum time not spent).
fix: Add server-side validation: if videoProgress < videoLength, reject videoCompleted=true. If timeSpent < minimumTime, reject isCompleted=true.

title: Discount always treated as absolute amount (acknowledged TODO)
description: Line 176 comment: 'TODO: F054 - discount is always treated as absolute amount. Add discountType field'. Percentage-based discounts are common in invoicing and this missing feature limits accounting accuracy.
fix: Add discountType enum (PERCENTAGE, FIXED) to the schema and update calculation logic.

title: VoIP settings uses spread operator on request body
description: Line 54: `const updated = { ...current, ...body }` spreads the entire request body into settings. An attacker with admin access could inject unexpected fields that get persisted to the database.
fix: Explicitly pick allowed fields from body instead of spreading: `const updated = { ...current, sipDomain: body.sipDomain, ... }`

title: Platform license management lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/platform/licenses manages per-seat billing for tenant employees. It uses auth() but has no CSRF validation. A CSRF attack could add or remove employee licenses, directly affecting billing.
fix: Add CSRF validation to the POST handler.

title: Platform module activation lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/platform/modules activates/deactivates SaaS modules for a tenant. No CSRF validation means a CSRF attack could activate paid modules, potentially creating unexpected Stripe charges.
fix: Add CSRF validation to module activation endpoints.

title: Subscription management lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/platform/subscription manages plan changes. No CSRF means an attacker could change a tenant's subscription plan via CSRF, potentially downgrading to lose features or upgrading to increase costs.
fix: Add CSRF validation.

title: Custom domain management lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/platform/domain manages custom domain configuration for tenants. No CSRF protection means an attacker could modify domain settings, potentially redirecting the tenant's custom domain.
fix: Add CSRF validation.

title: Billing portal session creation lacks CSRF protection
description: POST /api/platform/billing-portal creates Stripe billing portal sessions. No CSRF protection allows an attacker to redirect a tenant owner to a Stripe billing portal session they created.
fix: Add CSRF validation.

title: Public chatbot has 8 writes without transaction
description: The public chatbot endpoint performs 8 Prisma writes without $transaction. A failure mid-conversation could create orphaned messages or duplicate entries.
fix: Wrap conversation creation and message writes in $transaction.

title: Chat conversation handler has 6 writes without transaction
description: PUT/DELETE on chat conversations performs 6 writes without $transaction. Closing or deleting a conversation could leave related records (messages, assignments) in an inconsistent state.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Wishlist management has 6 writes without transaction
description: Wishlist create/update/delete performs 6 writes without $transaction.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Platform Stripe webhook has 5 writes without transaction
description: The Stripe webhook handler for platform events (subscription.created, updated, deleted) performs 5 writes without $transaction. If the tenant update succeeds but the audit log fails, there's no rollback.
fix: Wrap webhook processing writes in $transaction.

title: Mailing list unsubscribe has 6 writes without transaction
description: The unsubscribe flow performs 6 writes (preference update, token invalidation, audit log, etc.) without $transaction.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Email flows cron has 7 writes without transaction
description: The email flow execution cron performs 7 writes without $transaction. If email sending succeeds but the flow step update fails, the same email could be re-sent on the next cron run.
fix: Wrap each flow step execution in $transaction.

title: Auth profile returns 'User not found' (minor enumeration)
description: Line 15: Returns 'User not found' with 404 status. While this is behind authentication, it distinguishes between invalid sessions and deleted users, which is minor information disclosure.
fix: Return a generic 401 'Session invalid' instead of revealing that the user doesn't exist.

title: Multiple acknowledged TODOs for critical improvements not addressed
description: Found 10+ TODO comments in accounting routes for critical improvements: cursor-based pagination (reconciliation), aggregate SQL for OOM prevention (gst-qst), streaming exports, discountType field, bank-import route deprecation. These represent known debt.
fix: Create tickets for each TODO and prioritize based on data size risk.

title: TodoMaster proxy routes hardcode localhost URL
description: Three TodoMaster routes use `process.env.TODOMASTER_URL || 'http://localhost:8002'`. If the env var is not set in production, API calls to TodoMaster will fail silently or connect to localhost.
fix: Require TODOMASTER_URL env var in production or disable the routes when not configured.

title: Batch import skips CSRF for multipart/form-data
description: Line 162: `}, { skipCsrf: true }); // Skip CSRF for multipart/form-data file upload`. While CSRF tokens in multipart forms are tricky, completely skipping CSRF removes protection against malicious file uploads via CSRF.
fix: Include CSRF token as a form field in multipart uploads rather than skipping validation entirely.

title: Stripe webhook warns about missing secret but continues processing
description: Line 28: `logger.warn('Stripe Attitudes webhook secret not configured, skipping signature verification')`. In production, if the webhook secret env var is missing, the route processes unsigned webhooks, defeating the purpose of webhook verification.
fix: In production, return 503 if webhook secret is not configured. Only allow unverified webhooks in development.

title: V1 orders POST loads all products without limit
description: Line 164: `const products = await prisma.product.findMany({ where: { id: { in: productIds } } })` loads products without select clause, fetching all fields. For large catalogs, this is wasteful.
fix: Add select clause to only fetch needed fields: `select: { id: true, name: true, price: true, stock: true }`

title: Customer statements loads all invoices without pagination
description: Line 180: `prisma.customerInvoice.findMany({...})` without take/limit. For customers with hundreds of invoices, this could be slow.
fix: Add pagination or date range filtering.

title: Aurelia chat route in mutation list without CSRF
description: POST /api/aurelia/chat/respond accepts mutations without CSRF protection. If this route processes user messages to an AI assistant, CSRF could be used to inject messages on behalf of a user.
fix: Add authentication and CSRF validation.

title: V1 orders POST lacks idempotency
description: POST /api/v1/orders creates orders but has no idempotency key support. Network retries could create duplicate orders. The CORS allowed headers include x-idempotency-key but no route actually processes it.
fix: Accept and validate x-idempotency-key header. Store key with order and check for duplicates before creating.

title: VoIP queue management has 4 writes without transaction and no CSRF
description: VoIP queue CRUD performs 4 writes without $transaction and no CSRF. While behind auth(), modifying call queues affects business operations.
fix: Add CSRF and wrap writes in $transaction.

title: VoIP campaign management has 4 writes without transaction and no CSRF
description: VoIP campaign CRUD performs 4 writes without $transaction and no CSRF protection.
fix: Add CSRF and wrap writes in $transaction.

title: VoIP IVR management has 4 writes without transaction and no CSRF
description: IVR menu CRUD performs 4 writes without $transaction and no CSRF protection. IVR changes affect incoming call routing.
fix: Add CSRF and wrap writes in $transaction.

title: Company route has 5 writes without transaction
description: Company profile update performs 5 writes without $transaction.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Category management has 4 writes without transaction
description: Category CRUD performs 4 writes without $transaction.
fix: Wrap in prisma.$transaction().

title: Click tracking logs full URL (potential PII leakage)
description: Line 183: `logger.debug('[click-tracking] Click recorded', { emailLogId, url: clickedUrl.slice(0, 100) })`. URLs may contain PII via query parameters (email, tokens, user IDs).
fix: Strip query parameters from logged URLs: `url: new URL(clickedUrl).pathname`

title: Product types stored in filesystem JSON instead of database
description: Product types are stored in a JSON file on disk (data/product-types.json) instead of the database. This won't work on Azure App Service (ephemeral filesystem) and has no tenant isolation.
fix: Migrate product types to database storage with proper tenant isolation.

title: Debug endpoint marked for deletion still present
description: The comment says 'DELETE THIS after debugging is complete' but the endpoint remains in production code, exposing Telnyx API key prefixes and connection IDs.
fix: Delete the entire file src/app/api/voip/debug/route.ts.

title: Bank import file type validation is incomplete
description: The allowed MIME types list includes 'application/vnd.ms-excel' which could match XLS files, not just CSV. Also, files with .csv extension bypass MIME validation entirely (line 33-34).
fix: Validate file content (check for CSV structure) rather than relying solely on MIME type and extension.

title: callbackUrl in middleware redirect uses pathname without validation
description: Lines 420, 437: `url.searchParams.set('callbackUrl', pathname)` sets callbackUrl from the current pathname. While this isn't a direct open redirect (it uses pathname not user input), the downstream handling of callbackUrl on the sign-in page should validate it's a relative path.
fix: Ensure the sign-in page validates callbackUrl is a relative path starting with '/' and not an absolute URL.

title: CRM form redirectUrl allows arbitrary URLs without validation
description: Line 147: `if (j.data && j.data.redirectUrl) window.location.href = j.data.redirectUrl` redirects to a user-configured URL after form submission. If the form creator sets a malicious redirectUrl, form submitters could be redirected to phishing sites.
fix: Validate that redirectUrl is a same-origin URL or from an allowed domain list.

title: Invoice print uses setTimeout for window.print timing
description: Line 1106: `if (w) setTimeout(() => w.print(), 1000)` uses a fixed 1-second delay before printing. On slow connections or complex invoices, the content may not be loaded. On fast connections, the delay is unnecessary.
fix: Use the `load` event: `w.onload = () => w.print()` instead of a fixed timeout.
